1	// Package perf provides a reusable GraphQL performance harness for the DDx
2	// server. It seeds realistic on-disk fixtures (projects × beads, docgraph,
3	// agent sessions) using the real storage primitives, then drives each GraphQL
4	// query shape through both the in-process StateProvider and the full HTTP
5	// handler while recording p50/p95/p99 latencies.
6	//
7	// The harness exists so performance work can be grounded in numbers rather
8	// than anecdote (ddx-9ce6842a Part 1). New query shapes are added by
9	// appending to the target table in harness.go — the fixture and measurement
10	// layers stay unchanged.
11	package perf
12	
13	import (
14		"fmt"
15		"net/http"
16		"os"
17		"path/filepath"
18		"runtime"
19		"testing"
20		"time"
21	
22		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
23		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
24		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server"
25	)
26	
27	// BeadFixtureSpec describes the shape of a generated workspace.
28	//
29	// The defaults (10 projects × 500 beads = 5,000 beads) match the ddx-9ce6842a
30	// acceptance criteria §5, and the per-query floor of ≥1,000 records documented
31	// in the 2026-04-22 user directive on fixture size. Consumers that want a
32	// heavier run should raise BeadsPerProject rather than Projects so the
33	// distribution stays comparable.
34	type BeadFixtureSpec struct {
35		// Projects is the number of registered projects. Must be ≥ 1.
36		Projects int
37		// BeadsPerProject is the number of beads seeded in each project.
38		// Combined with Projects this yields the total bead corpus size.
39		BeadsPerProject int
40		// ClosedRatio (0..1) controls the fraction of seeded beads written with
41		// status=closed. Defaults to 0.3. A realistic mix matters because
42		// status=open filters are the common fast-path.
43		ClosedRatio float64
44		// LabelDensity is the number of labels assigned per bead. Defaults to 2.
45		LabelDensity int
46		// AgentSessionsPerProject is the number of agent-session rows seeded into
47		// each project's sessions.jsonl file. 0 means "do not seed sessions" —
48		// the sessions harness target will still run but report against the
49		// naturally-empty feed.
50		AgentSessionsPerProject int
51		// DocGraphFilesPerProject is the number of stub markdown files seeded
52		// into each project so the DocGraph target exercises real IO. 0 means
53		// the harness uses only whatever files already exist under the server's
54		// working directory.
55		DocGraphFilesPerProject int
56	}
57	
58	// DefaultBeadFixtureSpec is the baseline fixture: 10 projects × 500 beads
59	// (5,000 total) with a modest amount of docgraph + sessions content. This is
60	// what `make bench-graphql` runs and what baseline reports compare against.
61	func DefaultBeadFixtureSpec() BeadFixtureSpec {
62		return BeadFixtureSpec{
63			Projects:                10,
64			BeadsPerProject:         500,
65			ClosedRatio:             0.3,
66			LabelDensity:            2,
67			AgentSessionsPerProject: 100, // 10 × 100 = 1,000 session rows
68			DocGraphFilesPerProject: 4,
69		}
70	}
71	
72	// BeadFixture is a materialised on-disk workspace plus a live server.Server
73	// bound to it. Callers drive queries through Handler / State.
74	type BeadFixture struct {
75		Spec          BeadFixtureSpec
76		Server        *server.Server
77		Handler       http.Handler
78		Projects      []server.ProjectEntry
79		TargetProject server.ProjectEntry
80		TargetBeadID  string
81		BeadIDsByProj map[string][]string
82	}
83	
84	// TotalBeads returns the aggregate bead count across all seeded projects.
85	func (f *BeadFixture) TotalBeads() int {
86		return f.Spec.Projects * f.Spec.BeadsPerProject
87	}
88	
89	// BuildBeadFixture seeds the described workspace and returns a live fixture.
90	// It registers every project with the server's state so cross-project queries
91	// see all of them. The fixture is torn down automatically via tb.Cleanup.
92	func BuildBeadFixture(tb testing.TB, spec BeadFixtureSpec) *BeadFixture {
93		tb.Helper()
94		if spec.Projects < 1 {
95			tb.Fatalf("perf: fixture needs at least 1 project, got %d", spec.Projects)
96		}
97		if spec.BeadsPerProject < 1 {
98			tb.Fatalf("perf: fixture needs at least 1 bead per project, got %d", spec.BeadsPerProject)
99		}
100		if spec.LabelDensity <= 0 {
101			spec.LabelDensity = 1
102		}
103		if spec.ClosedRatio < 0 {
104			spec.ClosedRatio = 0
105		}
106		if spec.ClosedRatio > 1 {
107			spec.ClosedRatio = 1
108		}
109	
110		// Keep state out of the real user XDG dir so tests are hermetic.
111		tb.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", tb.TempDir())
112		tb.Setenv("DDX_NODE_NAME", "ddx-perf-harness")
113	
114		root := tb.TempDir()
115		projectPaths := make([]string, 0, spec.Projects)
116		beadIDsByProj := make(map[string][]string, spec.Projects)
117		var targetBeadID string
118	
119		for pi := 0; pi < spec.Projects; pi++ {
120			projectPath := filepath.Join(root, fmt.Sprintf("project-%02d", pi))
121			beads := seedProjectBeads(tb, projectPath, pi, spec)
122			ids := make([]string, len(beads))
123			for i, b := range beads {
124				ids[i] = b.ID
125			}
126			beadIDsByProj[projectPath] = ids
127			if spec.DocGraphFilesPerProject > 0 {
128				seedProjectDocGraph(tb, projectPath, pi, spec.DocGraphFilesPerProject)
129			}
130			if spec.AgentSessionsPerProject > 0 {
131				seedProjectSessions(tb, projectPath, pi, spec.AgentSessionsPerProject)
132			}
133			projectPaths = append(projectPaths, projectPath)
134			if pi == spec.Projects-1 && len(beads) > 0 {
135				// Target the final project's final bead so GetBeadSnapshot must
136				// walk past the other (spec.Projects - 1) projects on the slow
137				// path — this is what ddx-ad0db8fd cared about.
138				targetBeadID = beads[len(beads)-1].ID
139			}
140		}
141	
142		srv := server.New(":0", projectPaths[0])
143		tb.Cleanup(func() { _ = srv.Shutdown() })
144		for _, p := range projectPaths[1:] {
145			srv.RegisterProject(p)
146		}
147	
148		projects := srv.State().GetProjects()
149		if len(projects) != spec.Projects {
150			tb.Fatalf("perf: registered %d projects, expected %d", len(projects), spec.Projects)
151		}
152		var targetProject server.ProjectEntry
153		for _, proj := range projects {
154			if proj.Path == projectPaths[len(projectPaths)-1] {
155				targetProject = proj
156				break
157			}
158		}
159		if targetProject.ID == "" {
160			tb.Fatalf("perf: target project was not registered")
161		}
162	
163		return &BeadFixture{
164			Spec:          spec,
165			Server:        srv,
166			Handler:       srv.Handler(),
167			Projects:      projects,
168			TargetProject: targetProject,
169			TargetBeadID:  targetBeadID,
170			BeadIDsByProj: beadIDsByProj,
171		}
172	}
173	
174	func seedProjectBeads(tb testing.TB, projectPath string, projectIdx int, spec BeadFixtureSpec) []bead.Bead {
175		tb.Helper()
176		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectPath, ".ddx"))
177		if err := store.Init(); err != nil {
178			tb.Fatalf("perf: init bead store: %v", err)
179		}
180		baseTime := time.Date(2026, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
181		beads := make([]bead.Bead, 0, spec.BeadsPerProject)
182		closedCutoff := int(float64(spec.BeadsPerProject) * spec.ClosedRatio)
183		for bi := 0; bi < spec.BeadsPerProject; bi++ {
184			id := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-perf-%02d-%04d", projectIdx, bi)
185			now := baseTime.Add(time.Duration(projectIdx*spec.BeadsPerProject+bi) * time.Second)
186			status := bead.StatusOpen
187			if bi < closedCutoff {
188				status = bead.StatusClosed
189			}
190			labels := make([]string, 0, spec.LabelDensity)
191			for li := 0; li < spec.LabelDensity; li++ {
192				labels = append(labels, fmt.Sprintf("label-%d", (bi+li)%7))
193			}
194			labels = append(labels, fmt.Sprintf("project-%02d", projectIdx))
195			beads = append(beads, bead.Bead{
196				ID:          id,
197				Title:       fmt.Sprintf("Perf fixture bead %02d/%04d", projectIdx, bi),
198				Status:      status,
199				Priority:    bead.DefaultPriority,
200				IssueType:   bead.DefaultType,
201				CreatedAt:   now,
202				UpdatedAt:   now,
203				Labels:      labels,
204				Description: "GraphQL perf fixture — realistic-shape bead with a multi-line description so parsing costs reflect typical payloads rather than artificially small bodies.",
205			})
206		}
207		if err := store.WriteAll(beads); err != nil {
208			tb.Fatalf("perf: seed bead store: %v", err)
209		}
210		return beads
211	}
212	
213	func seedProjectDocGraph(tb testing.TB, projectPath string, projectIdx, count int) {
214		tb.Helper()
215		docsDir := filepath.Join(projectPath, "docs", "helix", "01-frame", "features")
216		if err := os.MkdirAll(docsDir, 0o755); err != nil {
217			tb.Fatalf("perf: mkdir docgraph: %v", err)
218		}
219		for di := 0; di < count; di++ {
220			name := fmt.Sprintf("FEAT-%02d-%03d.md", projectIdx, di)
221			path := filepath.Join(docsDir, name)
222			body := fmt.Sprintf("---\nid: FEAT-%02d-%03d\ntitle: Perf fixture feature\n---\n\n# FEAT-%02d-%03d\n\nFixture body line 1\nFixture body line 2\n", projectIdx, di, projectIdx, di)
223			if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(body), 0o644); err != nil {
224				tb.Fatalf("perf: write docgraph file: %v", err)
225			}
226		}
227	}
228	
229	func seedProjectSessions(tb testing.TB, projectPath string, projectIdx, count int) {
230		tb.Helper()
231		logDir := agent.SessionLogDirForWorkDir(projectPath)
232		base := time.Date(2026, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
233		for si := 0; si < count; si++ {
234			started := base.Add(time.Duration(projectIdx*count+si) * time.Minute)
235			entry := agent.SessionIndexEntry{
236				ID:         fmt.Sprintf("sess-%02d-%05d", projectIdx, si),
237				ProjectID:  agent.ProjectIDForPath(projectPath),
238				BeadID:     fmt.Sprintf("ddx-perf-%02d-%04d", projectIdx, si%10),
239				Harness:    "claude",
240				Surface:    "execute-bead",
241				Model:      "claude-sonnet-4-6",
242				StartedAt:  started,
243				EndedAt:    started.Add(time.Duration(1000+si*10) * time.Millisecond),
244				DurationMS: 1000 + si*10,
245				Tokens:     1000 + si,
246				Outcome:    "success",
247				Effort:     "medium",
248			}
249			if err := agent.AppendSessionIndex(logDir, entry, started); err != nil {
250				tb.Fatalf("perf: write session index row: %v", err)
251			}
252		}
253	}
254	
255	// Environment returns a short footer describing the machine the benchmark ran
256	// on. Included verbatim in the baseline report (ddx-9ce6842a AC §2).
257	func Environment() string {
258		return fmt.Sprintf("go=%s goos=%s goarch=%s cpu-cores=%d", runtime.Version(), runtime.GOOS, runtime.GOARCH, runtime.NumCPU())
259	}

data.text:
Committed as `f912445`. The change adds the missing 24h sparkline to the unified endpoints list (the remaining BLOCK from prior reviews):

- `ProviderStatus.sparkline: [Int!]!` — 24 hourly token totals computed from the sessions index, suppressed (empty) when fewer than 6 non-zero buckets exist
- `+page.svelte` renders a 24-bar inline trend column per row, gated on `length >= 6`
- Resolver wiring in `providerStatusesFromInfos`, `HarnessStatuses`, and `providerRowsWithUsage`; `cloneProviderRows` now deep-copies the slice
- Two new unit tests (`TestBuildSparkline*`) and Playwright assertions on `endpoint-sparkline-bars-*`

Other AC items from the prior review chain were already addressed in `c334d94e` and earlier (polling for live patching, async `defaultRouteStatus`, nullable usage, `LookupHarnessRateLimit`→`QuotaFromRateLimitSignal` bridge, chart ceiling overlay, server-side seeded-fixture trend test, perf fixtures ≥1k rows / ≥5 endpoints, design one-pager).
